title: Mobile Device Security: MDM, BYOD, and Mobile Threat Defense
content:
# Mobile Device Security: MDM, BYOD, and Mobile Threat Defense

## Overview

Mobile devices represent a significant and expanding attack surface for organizations. Smartphones and tablets access corporate email, applications, and data while operating outside traditional network perimeters. This guide covers Mobile Device Management (MDM), Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) security, and Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) from a SOC operations perspective.

SOC analysts must understand mobile-specific threats, the telemetry available from mobile management platforms, and investigation procedures for mobile device compromise.

## Mobile Threat Landscape

| Threat Category | Examples | Risk Level |
|----------------|----------|------------|
| Malicious applications | Trojanized apps, spyware, stalkerware | High |
| Phishing (smishing/vishing) | SMS-based phishing, voice phishing, QR code phishing | High |
| Network attacks | Rogue WiFi, man-in-the-middle, SSL stripping | Medium |
| Device exploitation | OS vulnerability exploit, jailbreak/root exploits | Critical |
| Data leakage | Unauthorized cloud backup, clipboard sharing, screenshot capture | Medium |
| Physical access | Device theft, USB debugging exploitation, juice jacking | Medium |
| Configuration vulnerabilities | No passcode, outdated OS, developer mode enabled | Medium |

## MDM Security Capabilities

### Core MDM Functions for Security

| Function | Description | SOC Relevance |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| Device enrollment | Managed device registration and configuration | Track managed vs. unmanaged devices |
| Policy enforcement | Passcode, encryption, OS version requirements | Compliance monitoring |
| App management | Approved app catalog, app restrictions, MAM | Malicious app prevention |
| Remote actions | Lock, wipe, locate device | Incident response actions |
| Compliance monitoring | Continuous assessment against security policies | Alert on non-compliant devices |
| Certificate management | Push certificates for VPN, WiFi, email | Authentication security |
| Content management | Managed documents, secure container | Data protection |

### MDM Security Policies to Monitor

| Policy | Recommended Setting | Alert Condition |
|--------|-------------------|----------------|
| Passcode required | 6+ digit or alphanumeric | Passcode disabled or weakened |
| Biometric authentication | Enabled where supported | Biometric disabled after being enabled |
| Device encryption | Required (enabled by default on modern devices) | Encryption disabled |
| Minimum OS version | Current major version -1 | Device below minimum version for >30 days |
| Jailbreak/root detection | Block access | Jailbroken/rooted device detected |
| Screen lock timeout | 5 minutes maximum | Timeout extended beyond policy |
| Camera restrictions | Per policy (secure areas) | Camera enabled in restricted zone |
| USB debugging | Disabled | Developer options enabled on managed device |
| Unknown sources (Android) | Disabled | Sideloading enabled |
| App allowlist/blocklist | Enforced | Blocked app installed |

### MDM Alert Triage

When the MDM platform generates a compliance alert:

```
MDM Compliance Alert Received
  |
  +-- Device jailbroken/rooted?
  |   |
  |   +-- YES --> P1: Immediately revoke corporate access,
  |   |           notify device owner, initiate investigation
  |   +-- NO --> Continue triage
  |
  +-- Corporate data at risk? (encryption disabled, passcode removed)
  |   |
  |   +-- YES --> P2: Notify device owner, set 24-hour compliance deadline,
  |   |           block access if not remediated
  |   +-- NO --> Continue triage
  |
  +-- OS critically outdated? (known exploited vulnerabilities)
  |   |
  |   +-- YES --> P2: Notify device owner, set 72-hour update deadline
  |   +-- NO --> P3: Standard compliance notification workflow
  |
  +-- Log all compliance events for trend analysis
```

## BYOD Security Framework

### BYOD Risk Assessment

| Factor | Corporate-Owned | BYOD | Risk Delta |
|--------|----------------|------|------------|
| Device management | Full MDM control | Limited MDM or MAM only | Higher risk -- less visibility |
| App control | Full allowlist/blocklist | MAM container apps only | Higher risk -- unmanaged apps |
| Data separation | Device-level encryption, full wipe | Container encryption, selective wipe | Medium risk -- data commingling |
| OS updates | Enforced through MDM | Encouraged, cannot force | Higher risk -- delayed patching |
| Jailbreak/root detection | Enforced, device blocked | Detected, access limited | Similar if detection is robust |
| Lost device response | Full remote wipe | Selective wipe of corporate data only | Higher risk -- PI remains |
| Privacy | Organization controls device | User privacy concerns limit monitoring | Higher risk -- less telemetry |

### BYOD Security Architecture

**Option 1: MDM with Work Profile (Recommended for Android)**
- Android Enterprise work profile creates separate managed container
- Corporate apps and data isolated from personal
- IT manages work profile only; personal side private
- Selective wipe removes only work profile

**Option 2: MAM-Only (Mobile Application Management)**
- No device-level management
- Corporate apps wrapped with security policies
- App-level encryption, DLP, and authentication
- Suitable for highest BYOD privacy requirements
- Limited security telemetry

**Option 3: Virtual Mobile Infrastructure (VMI)**
- Corporate apps run on server-side virtual device
- Only screen pixels transmitted to personal device
- No corporate data stored on device
- Requires network connectivity
- Highest security, lowest user experience

## Mobile Threat Defense (MTD)

### MTD Capabilities

| Capability | Description | Detection Examples |
|-----------|-------------|--------------------|
| Device-level threats | OS exploitation, jailbreak, rooting | Known CVE exploitation, checkra1n, Magisk |
| App-level threats | Malicious apps, sideloaded apps, app vulnerabilities | Trojanized apps, apps with excessive permissions, known malicious SDKs |
| Network-level threats | Rogue access points, MitM, SSL interception | Evil twin WiFi, ARP spoofing, certificate pinning bypass |
| Phishing protection | SMS/URL phishing detection | Malicious URL blocking, SMS link scanning |
| Content threats | Malicious profiles, certificates | Rogue configuration profiles, untrusted CA certificates |

### MTD Alert Response

| Alert Type | Severity | Response |
|-----------|----------|----------|
| Jailbreak/root detected | Critical | Immediately revoke all corporate access. Do not wait for user response. |
| Known malware app installed | Critical | Block corporate access, push notification to user, request removal. |
| Man-in-the-middle attack detected | High | Disconnect from network, alert user, block corporate data sync. |
| Phishing URL accessed | High | Check if credentials were entered, force password reset if uncertain. |
| Sideloaded app detected | Medium | Alert user, assess app risk, block if on threat list. |
| Outdated OS with known exploit | Medium | Notify user, set remediation timeline, restrict access if unpatched. |
| Rogue WiFi connection | Medium | Alert user, verify no data interception, recommend VPN usage. |
| Suspicious app permissions | Low | Log for review, assess in context of app function. |

## Mobile Device Investigation

### iOS Investigation

**Available without MDM/forensic tool**:
```
# Check device information
Settings > General > About
- Serial Number, IMEI, Model, iOS Version

# Check installed profiles
Settings > General > VPN & Device Management
- List all configuration profiles and MDM enrollment

# Check for VPN configurations
Settings > General > VPN & Device Management > VPN

# Check screen time for app usage
Settings > Screen Time > See All App & Website Activity

# Check for unusual battery drain (indicator of background malicious activity)
Settings > Battery > Battery Usage by App
```

**With MDM console access**:
- Device details: OS version, model, serial, IMEI, last check-in
- Installed applications list with versions
- Configuration profiles applied
- Compliance status and violations
- Device location (if enabled and consented)
- Security status: encryption, passcode, jailbreak detection

**With forensic tool (Cellebrite, GrayKey, Axiom)**:
- Full file system extraction (if supported for device/OS version)
- Application data including databases and caches
- Communication records (SMS, iMessage, call logs)
- Location history
- Browser history and bookmarks
- Deleted data recovery (APFS snapshots)
- Keychain contents (device-level encryption key required)

### Android Investigation

**ADB-based investigation (if USB debugging enabled or authorized)**:
```bash
# List installed packages
adb shell pm list packages -f

# List packages installed from unknown sources (sideloaded)
adb shell pm list packages -i | grep -v "com.android.vending"

# Check device properties
adb shell getprop | grep -E "ro.build|ro.product|ro.hardware"

# Check if device is rooted
adb shell su -c "id" 2>/dev/null
adb shell which su 2>/dev/null
adb shell ls -la /system/app/Superuser.apk 2>/dev/null

# List running processes
adb shell ps -A

# Check network connections
adb shell netstat -tlnp

# Dump logcat for security events
adb logcat -d -b security
adb logcat -d -b events | grep -E "am_proc_start|am_proc_died"

# Extract application APK for analysis
adb shell pm path <package_name>
adb pull <apk_path>

# Check device encryption status
adb shell getprop ro.crypto.state

# List device administrators
adb shell dumpsys device_policy
```

**With MDM console access**:
- Similar capabilities to iOS MDM: device details, apps, compliance, location
- Android Enterprise work profile contents
- SafetyNet/Play Integrity attestation results
- Google Play Protect scan results

## Mobile Incident Response Playbook

### Compromised Mobile Device

1. **Containment (immediate)**
   - Revoke corporate account access (disable account or revoke OAuth tokens)
   - Remove device from corporate WiFi allowlist
   - Block device certificate in MDM
   - If MDM available: push compliance action to block corporate apps

2. **Assessment**
   - Review MDM telemetry: compliance history, app inventory changes, last check-in
   - Review MTD alerts: threat detections, network attacks, malicious apps
   - Review authentication logs: sign-ins from the device, location anomalies
   - Review email/DLP logs: data accessed or exfiltrated from the device

3. **Evidence Collection**
   - Export MDM device details and compliance history
   - Export MTD alert history for the device
   - Capture authentication logs showing device access
   - If physical access available: forensic image (coordinate with legal)

4. **Remediation**
   - If corporate-owned: remote wipe and re-enroll
   - If BYOD: selective wipe of corporate data
   - Reset all corporate credentials accessed from the device
   - Revoke and reissue device certificates
   - Review and revoke OAuth tokens for corporate applications

5. **Recovery**
   - Re-provision device with current security baseline
   - Re-enroll in MDM with updated policies
   - Verify compliance before restoring corporate access
   - Monitor for recurrence

### Lost or Stolen Device

1. **Immediate actions** (within 1 hour of report)
   - Remote lock via MDM
   - Initiate remote wipe if device contains sensitive data
   - Disable corporate accounts accessed from device
   - Revoke device certificates and OAuth tokens

2. **Follow-up** (within 24 hours)
   - Confirm remote wipe executed successfully
   - Reset all passwords for accounts accessed from device
   - Review access logs for unauthorized activity after loss/theft time
   - File police report if theft suspected
   - Notify compliance if regulated data was on device

## Mobile Security Metrics

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| MDM enrollment rate | >95% of corporate devices | Enrolled / total corporate devices |
| OS currency | >90% on supported versions | Devices on current-1 / total devices |
| Jailbreak/root detection rate | 0 compromised devices | Detected jailbroken devices |
| MTD deployment rate | >90% of managed devices | MTD active / total managed devices |
| Mean time to device wipe (lost/stolen) | <4 hours | Average time from report to wipe confirmation |
| BYOD compliance rate | >85% | Compliant BYOD devices / total BYOD |
| Mobile phishing click rate | <3% | Users clicking mobile phishing links / total users |

## References

- NIST SP 800-124 Rev 2: Guidelines for Managing the Security of Mobile Devices
- CIS Benchmarks for iOS and Android
- OWASP Mobile Security Testing Guide (MSTG)
- MITRE ATT&CK for Mobile: https://attack.mitre.org/matrices/mobile/
- NSA Mobile Device Best Practices
